channel.title: World
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from World, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Sainsbury’s to cut 300 jobs as it restructures tech team and Argos deliveries
item.description: Head office job losses part of plan for more separation between supermarket and Argos businesses Sainsbury’s is cutting 300 head office jobs as it res...

item.title: Digested week: ‘Just when you thought it was safe to go back in the water … ’ but this time, it’s real | Emma Brockes
item.description: Three-part docudrama makes notion of going into the sea in the UK terrifying – much as Jaws did for Americans in 1975 Since I was a child I’ve been go...

item.title: Science of sex and gender being misrepresented by Trump officials, experts warn
item.description: Scientists say crackdown on gender-affirming care could have impact on healthcare of all Americans As more health systems end gender-affirming care fo...

item.title: France condemns decision to provisionally apply divisive Mercosur trade deal – Europe live
item.description: Trade between the EU and two South American countries may start within two months under a provision application of the deal Trade between the EU and t...

item.title: ‘Our patience has now run out’: Pakistan declares ‘open war’ against Afghanistan after cross-border attack – live news
item.description: Pakistani forces launched airstrikes against military targets in the Afghan capital, Kabul, as well as other provinces close to the border Both sides ...

item.title: Man arrested after Winston Churchill statue defaced with graffiti
item.description: The statue of the former prime minster at Westminster was vandalised with slogans daubed in red paint.

item.title: Pakistan strikes Afghanistan and declares 'open war' on Taliban - what we know so far
item.description: Bombings by Pakistan are the most significant development in tensions between the two neighbours - though the physical impact remains unclear.

item.title: Why Trump means the Cuban Revolution faces its biggest threat yet
item.description: Will a worsening internal crisis create the conditions for the Cuban Revolution to unravel from within?

item.title: From plumber to Parliament, who is the Green Party's new MP?
item.description: Spencer marked her victory by apologising to customers for potentially having to cancel work due to her Westminster move.

item.title: Soham murderer Ian Huntley in serious condition after makeshift weapon attack
item.description: Huntley, who is serving a life sentence for murdering two schoolgirls, has significant head trauma, the BBC understands.
